Article Title: The hidden costs of excessive screen media use in kids
Article Text:
New research highlights how children in India exceed global

screen time guidelines

, with lasting impact on health and cognitive abilities

Multiple screens have become an integral part of children’s lives. But is it safe for young adolescents to be spending so much time on screen? Findings from existing research suggest that while devices like smartphones, laptops, tablets and TVs can aid learning, their excessive use is linked to adverse health and developmental outcomes.A recent study*, published in Indian Journal of Psychiatry, examined children and adolescents with mental disorders to assess their screen use habits. The findings are striking: Average daily screen time was 3.1 hours. Importantly, two-thirds of the 212 study participants (average age 13 years) exceeded the recommended screen time limits set by the American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) and World Health Organization (WHO).These guidelines recommend no screen exposure for children under two years (except video calls), less than one hour per day for ages two to four, and a maximum of two hours daily for those five years and older.The study revealed that television (66%) and mobile phones (70.3%) were the most commonly used devices, among 212 children and adolescents who were part of the study. Alarmingly, 22.2% of children met the criteria for screen media addiction, based on DSM-5 standards for Internet Gaming Disorder.DSM-5 is Diagnostics and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, fifth edition, a guide used by researchers to diagnose, classify and treat mental health disorders.Addiction was more prevalent among boys, those from joint or extended families, and children diagnosed with neurodevelopmental or disruptive disorders.Excessive screen use contributes to obesity, sedentary behaviour, poor dietary habits, and disrupted sleep. Cognitively, prolonged exposure can impair language development, executive functioning and emergent literacy skills. Research even suggests structural brain changes in preschool children with high screen exposure, particularly affecting white matter tracts (which act as brain’s communication networks), critical for learning and communication.Psychosocially, children addicted to screen media often display deception (sneaking or lying to use devices), preoccupation, withdrawal symptoms and conflicts within families. Screen use also interferes with friendships and family activities, while increasing vulnerability to cyberbullying, unsafe online content and negative emotional states.The study underscores the urgent need for parental awareness and regulation. Parents often allow children with neurodevelopmental disorders extra screen time as a coping mechanism, but this leads to more problems. In joint family settings, inconsistent parenting rules can further exacerbate the issue.In conclusion, screen media use among children is not inherently harmful, but its excessive and unregulated use poses significant risks to physical health, cognitive development and emotional well-being. With nearly one-fourth of children in psychiatric care showing signs of addiction, routine evaluation of screen habits and strict adherence to recommended guidelines are essential steps toward safeguarding the next generation.(* Problematic screen media use in children and adolescents attending child and adolescent psychiatric services in a tertiary care center in North India, by Venkatesh Raju, Akhilesh Sharma, Ruchita Shah, Ravikanth Tangella, Sana Devi Yumnam, Jyoti Singh, Jaivinder Yadav, and Sandeep Grover)
Article Summary: New research highlights how children in india exceed global screen time guidelines. Average daily screen time was 3.1 hours. Tv (66%) and mobile phones (70.3%) were the most commonly used devices. Alarmingly, 22.2% of children met the criteria for screen media addiction.

Article Title: The story of our times and the tesseract of truth
Article Text:
Also read:

“Tesseract: The Geometry of Truth” is produced by The Times of India, with concept and visualisation by Meera Jain.

Experience “Tesseract: The Geometry of Truth”, running from 16 to 22 March 2026 at

NCPA Mumbai

. Book

Time is the fourth dimension, in addition to the length, width and height of the usual spatial cognition of our world, though most may not realise its ubiety. It is what takes our square existence beyond the humdrum two-dimensional flatness, beyond even the stereoscopy of the cube to the refracting multifacetedness of the hyper-cube, the tesseract. Understanding the tesseract, then, is the key to understanding the story of us and deciding the way forward.Nowadays, when information is abundant but discernment is not, truth very often gets obscured. Only a detailed chronicling of time - our times - reveals the intricate contours of the tesseract, the geometry of truth, going beyond the 3D of what has been all-too-easily accepted as reality down the ages, or even the only reality.Time provides the crucial context, tracks and marks every change, and therefore determines the diverse perceptions of truth too.Humankind has been on a quest for truth even before the concept of time dawned on us. Beyond what was, what is and even what will be, though, is the fourth axis of 'what could'. It has always been there but not perceived until minds were awakened to its prescience. Transcending the three axes to the reflective fourth one, the tesseract, has become an imperative in our times of clashing truths and blinding contradictions that defy reason and confound the mind.A tesseract is complex but not complicated. As a metaphor for our space-time continuum, it is lucid and yet profound, liberating and illuminating, and yes, utterly mind-bending and mind-blowing in its vividness. When the curtain rises on The Tesseract: The Geometry of Truth at the National Centre for the Performing Arts (NCPA) in Mumbai today, take a journey into the story of our times and understand the clarion call to Let Truth Prevail.
Article Summary: Time is the fourth dimension, in addition to the length, width and height of the usual spatial cognition of our world. Only a detailed chronicling of time - our times - reveals the intricate contours of the tesseract, the geometry of truth. The tesseract: the geometry of truth runs from 16 to 22 march 2026 at the national centre for the performing arts (ncpa) in mumbai.

Article Title: Govt proposes to drop repeat virus tests on plasma-based medicines
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Centre has proposed amending drug rules to stop repeating virus tests on medicines made from human plasma, saying the raw plasma used to produce these products is already screened for infections such as HIV and hepatitis before manufacturing begins.These medicines include albumin, intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG), and clotting factors such as Factor VIII and Factor IX, which are used to treat immune disorders, severe infections and bleeding conditions such as haemophilia.Officials said the move aims to align India’s drug regulations with international pharmacopoeia standards. Global guidelines require pooled plasma to be tested for hepatitis B surface antigen, hepatitis C virus RNA and HIV antibodies before it is used for fractionation, and only plasma that tests negative is cleared for manufacturing plasma-derived medicines.Currently, plasma collected for manufacturing these medicines is first pooled and tested for viruses, including HIV, hepatitis B and hepatitis C. However, once medicines are produced from this screened plasma, the finished products are again tested for the same viral markers under existing rules. The government now proposes to remove this second round of testing.The health ministry has issued a draft notification seeking public comments on amendments to the Drugs Rules, 1945, which regulate the testing of blood-derived products.Dr Aseem Kumar Tiwari, Senior Director, Department of Transfusion Medicine, Medanta, Gurugram, said that surplus plasma collected from blood donors may be used by plasma fractionators to manufacture several life-saving medicines.“Plasma-derived medicinal products (PDMP) such as albumin, intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG), and clotting factors like Factor VIII and Factor IX are widely used to treat immune disorders, severe infections and bleeding conditions such as haemophilia,” he said.He added that blood centres often generate surplus plasma after meeting patient needs, which can be supplied to specialised fractionation facilities where different proteins are separated to manufacture these medicines.Dr Tiwari said PDMPs undergo multiple safety checks before reaching patients. “Donated plasma is screened for infections such as HIV, hepatitis B, hepatitis C, malaria, and syphilis, and the manufacturing process includes viral inactivation steps to ensure safety,” he said.“These medicines, known globally as plasma-derived medicinal products, have not been linked to infection transmission because of stringent testing and viral inactivation during manufacturing,” he added.Officials say repeating the same viral tests at the finished product stage creates duplication not required under global practices. The proposed amendment seeks to rationalise testing requirements while maintaining strict safety checks at the plasma screening stage.The draft rules were issued after consultation with the Drugs Technical Advisory Board, and stakeholders have been given 30 days to submit comments before the amendment is finalised.
Article Summary: Officials say the move aims to align india’s drug regulations with international pharmacopoeia standards. Currently, plasma collected for manufacturing these medicines is first pooled and tested for viruses, including hiv, hepatitis b and hepatitis c. once medicines are produced from this screened plasma, the finished products are again tested for the same viral markers.

Article Title: Isro tests CE20 cryo engine at 22-tonne thrust
Article Text: BENGALURU: Isro Thursday said it successfully conducted a sea-level hot test of its CE20 cryogenic engine at 22-tonne thrust at the Isro Propulsion Complex on March 10.The CE20 engine powers the upper cryogenic stage of the LVM-3 launch vehicle. The latest test was carried out using a nozzle protection system and a multi-element igniter.“Earlier, sea-level tests using the nozzle protection system were conducted at a thrust level of 19 tonnes. The latest trial qualifies sea-level testing of the engine at the higher 22-tonne thrust level. The test lasted 165 seconds,” Isro said.Isro plans to operate future LVM-3 missions with an uprated C32 cryogenic stage delivering 22-tonne thrust from the CE20 engine in order to enhance the rocket’s payload capability.“As a result, the flight acceptance test of the CE20 engine will also need to be conducted at the higher thrust level,” Isro said.The space agency said testing the CE20 engine at sea level poses significant challenges due to the engine’s high area-ratio nozzle, which has an exit pressure of about 50 mbar. A key concern during such tests is flow separation inside the nozzle, which can cause severe vibrations and thermal stress at the separation point and potentially damage the nozzle.According to Isro, the cryogenic engine used in the latest trial has undergone a record 20 successful hot tests. These tests have enabled the demonstration of several technologies using a single engine.“These include ignition using a multi-element igniter, ignition margin demonstration for Gaganyaan mission across a wide range of propellant tank pressures and pre-ignition chamber pressures, and engine qualification for Gaganyaan at 20-tonne thrust level,” Isro added.The tests also demonstrated and qualified operation at 22-tonne thrust, the boot-strap mode start of the CE20 engine without a start-up system to enable in-flight restart, qualification of indigenous turbopump bearings and sensors, and qualification of the nozzle protection system for hot tests of a high area-ratio nozzle at sea level.Isro said the performance of the engine and the test facility remained as expected throughout the test duration.
Article Summary: Isro said it successfully conducted a sea-level hot test of its ce20 cryogenic engine at 22-tonne thrust. The ce20 engine powers the upper cryogenic stage of the lvm-3 launch vehicle. Isro plans to operate future missions with an uprated c32 cryogenic stage.

Article Title: Sara Gonzales: Janitors, ice cream sales analyst: Texas whistleblower reveals 'craziest H-1B job titles'
Article Text:
India Flags H-1B Visa Delays With US As Indians Face Backlogs Scrutiny And Policy Uncertainty





Texas journalist and whistleblower Sara Gonzales, in her latest YouTube video, revealed a list of H-1B job titles that she found 'crazy' and said that it is not only Indian software engineers 'exploiting' the H-1B visa program, but there are also others. Gonzales started her own investigation into the H-1B employers as she visited some of the hirers and found empty offices and home addresses.As Gonzales continues her investigation, she has now come up with a list of job titles that, according to her, should not require a foreign national with any special skills. Gonzales said the list busts the narrative that companies are forced to bring in foreign nationals for these roles."Why the hell do we need to hire janitors from a foreign country?" Gonzales said, citing the record of a Massachusetts cleaning service hiring janitors for a salary of $31,200. "They can't find anyone in the country who knows a thing about cleaning toilets," Gonzales said.The list that the whistleblower presented included a retail associate, a dry cleaning worker, public relations specialist, travel agent, an HR manager for a Mexican restaurant, an HR manager for a smokeshop in Texas and an ice cream sales analyst in Chicago.Gonzales mocked that in an obese America, they could not find anyone for ice cream sales analyst.The YouTuber also cited a record of a California business called 'Sing in Chinese' hiring foreigners on H-1B for the position of English teacher. "Yes, they could not find an English teacher in America," Gonzales added.The H-1B visa program allows companies to hire foreigners for specialty occupations with skills that are not present in Americans. Silicon Valley companies are highly dependent of this visa program as they hire engineers from India and China but Gonzales revealed how small cleaning services companies are also hiring foreigners and are contributing to building an eco system that needs to hire foreigner English teachers for the non-English speaking immigrant workers.The visa program has now come under immense scrutiny as MAGA called for an abolition of this program, as they are stealing jobs from Americans. The administration imposed a $100,000 fee each foreign national a company hires on H-1B, and the lottery system has also been overhauled.
Article Summary: Texas journalist and whistleblower sara gonzales, in her latest youtube video, revealed a list of h-1b job titles that she found 'crazy' gonzales said the list busts the narrative that companies are forced to bring in foreign nationals for these roles. The visa program has now come under immense scrutiny as maga called for an abolition of this program.

Article Title: Leanna Lenee Cheated Rumors: A viral video claims Leanna Lenee cheated on Travis Hunter and took $200K in alimony — here’s the truth
Article Text:
Travis Hunter and his wife Leanna Lenee (Image via Indtagram)

Did Leanna Lenee Cheat on Travis Hunter and Take $200K in Alimony? Here’s the Truth







Social media’s obsession with Colorado Buffaloes star Travis Hunter and his wife Leanna Lenee is unbelievable. Ever since the couple started dating and eventually got married, some people have constantly pushed narratives about them separating. In the latest round of rumors circulating online, claims suggest that Hunter was cheated on by his wife because she refused to sign a prenup and is now allegedly bragging about receiving $200,000 a month in alimony while vacationing in Cancun with a new partner.It’s a story designed to make you angry but there’s one major problem: None of it is true.To understand why this story is false, we have to look at the actual facts of Travis Hunter’s life versus the fiction created by "rage-bait" accounts.Travis Hunter and Leanna Lenee have been in a high-profile, committed relationship for years, but they recently got legally married. People had a major problem with them not having a prenup and so the narrative started. The account that posted this, "Obsrvate," is a known satire page. These accounts thrive on creating "fake news" that looks real enough to trick casual scrollers.There is no video, tweet, or Instagram story where Leanna claimed she "got what she wanted."In reality, the couple continues to appear together on their joint YouTube channel and social media, showing a united front.The old video of the couple has been used in the wrong context to fuel an already existing rumor that the internet wanted to believe wholeheartedly.Why do these stories spread so fast? This is a classic example of Rage-Bait. These posts are engineered to trigger a strong emotional response. By painting a picture of a hard-working athlete being "taken advantage of," the post ensures that people will comment in anger, share it with friends, and boost the post’s reach.The goal isn't to report the news, it's to use the names of famous people to generate engagement revenue for the account holder.In the era of AI and viral "parody" accounts, it is easier than ever to fall for a convincing lie. Travis Hunter remains focused on his record-breaking football career, and his relationship with Leanna Lenee remains a private matter that has been wildly misrepresented for internet "likes."Before hitting the share button on the next celebrity "scandal," it’s always worth checking the source. In this case, the only thing being "exposed" is the misinformation itself.
Article Summary: Travis hunter and leanna lenee have been in a high-profile, committed relationship for years. People had a major problem with them not having a prenup and so the narrative started. The couple continues to appear together on their joint youtube channel and social media, showing a united front.

Article Title: Trey Hendrickson Net Worth: How Trey Hendrickson’s $112M Ravens deal will boost his net worth in 2026
Article Text:
(Image via: Getty Images)

Inside Trey Hendrickson’s net worth after landing a massive $112M Ravens deal

Immediate Wealth: The deal includes a $20 million signing bonus paid upfront.



Guaranteed Security: Hendrickson secured $60 million in full guarantees. This was the sticking point in Cincinnati; the Ravens have ensured he gets paid regardless of injury or performance over the next two seasons.



Annual Salary: At an average of $28 million per year, Hendrickson now ranks among the top 10 highest-paid edge rushers in NFL history.



Calculating the 2026 Net Worth





Trey Hendrickson is no longer just one of the NFL’s most feared pass rushers, he is officially one of its highest-paid. On March 11, 2026, Hendrickson shook the league by signing a massive four-year, $112 million contract with the Baltimore Ravens.For a player who spent years locked in a contract standoff with the Cincinnati Bengals, this deal is a life-changing "win." Here is a look at what this means for his net worth and his career.The new contract is a masterpiece of financial security. While the base value is $112 million, performance incentives can push the total to $120 million.To understand Hendrickson’s net worth by the end of 2026, we have to look at his "Career Cash."Before this deal, Hendrickson had already earned roughly $97 million across nine seasons with the Saints and Bengals. By the time the 2026 season concludes, his total career earnings will soar to approximately $123 million.While his private net worth is affected by taxes (roughly 40-50%) and agent fees, his "liquid" wealth will take a massive jump this year. He is effectively doubling his career earnings potential in a single afternoon of negotiations.Baltimore’s move wasn't just about money; it was about timing. The Ravens had initially agreed to a trade for Maxx Crosby, but the deal collapsed when Crosby failed his physical.The Ravens pivoted instantly to Hendrickson, who was the #1 ranked free agent on the market. Despite missing time in 2025 with a core muscle injury, his track record is undeniable: 35 sacks between 2023 and 2024. Baltimore is betting $120 million that his "relentless motor" is the missing piece for their championship run.Trey Hendrickson’s journey from a third-round pick to a $100M+ superstar is a testament to betting on yourself. By 2027, he will likely have more "cash in hand" than almost any defensive player in the history of the AFC North.
Article Summary: Trey hendrickson signed a massive four-year, $112 million contract with the baltimore ravens. Hendrickson now ranks among the top 10 highest-paid edge rushers in nfl history. By 2027, he will likely have more "cash in hand" than almost any defensive player in the afc north.

Article Title: Ja'Marr Chase Contract: Ja’Marr Chase got his $161 million bag, but is the $69 million receiver gamble why they'll never win a Super Bowl?
Article Text:
Ja’Marr Chase reset the NFL receiver market, but the real debate is whether the Bengals paid too much at one position. (Image via Getty)

Why the “overpaid” debate around Ja’Marr Chase is really about the Bengals’ roster math

Why Ja’Marr Chase’s net worth still looks low next to that massive contract





Ja’Marr Chase did not just reset the wide receiver market in March 2025. He blew past it. The Cincinnati Bengals gave him a four-year, $161 million extension with $112 million guaranteed, making him the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history at $40.25 million per year.That number is why the debate has not stopped. Chase is coming off a receiving triple crown season, so paying him was never the shocking part. The real argument is whether Cincinnati committed too much money to one position, especially after also paying Tee Higgins and pushing the Bengals’ receiver spending to roughly $69 million per year.Let’s be clear. Based on the information available here, Chase himself is not the weak link in this conversation.He is an elite receiver in his prime, and the market was always going to move after Justin Jefferson signed for $35 million per year. Chase pushed it to $40.25 million because that is what elite young stars do when they hit extension time.The louder criticism is really about Cincinnati’s team-building. Chase’s deal was structured with rolling guarantees and backloading, which reportedly saved the Bengals more than $15 million in immediate cap space. That softens the short-term blow. But it does not erase the bigger question: can a team pay this much to two receivers and still build a complete roster around Joe Burrow?That is where the “overpaid” label started showing up. It was aimed more directly at Higgins.In March 2025, CBS Sports cited Bleacher Report’s Brad Gagnon calling Higgins Cincinnati’s most overpaid player after his four-year deal worth up to $115 million. Then in September 2025, The Sporting News again highlighted Higgins as overpaid, not Chase.That matters. The criticism in the coverage you shared is not that Chase is a fraud. It is that the Bengals may have boxed themselves in by spending so heavily at wide receiver.Big contract does not automatically mean giant net worth overnight. Parade reported in September 2025 that Chase’s net worth was estimated at around $25 million, citing Marca and ClutchPoints. That number looks modest next to a $161 million extension, but it makes sense once you look at the timeline.Before this deal, Chase was still playing on his rookie contract after signing a four-year deal worth close to $31 million in 2021. His new extension also is not paid out all at once. Parade reported he is set to make about $23.57 million in 2025, then $26.23 million in 2026, $33.4 million in 2027, and $41.4 million in 2028, with a fifth-year option around $53.22 million in 2029.So yes, Chase is now paid like a quarterback. But his net worth has not fully caught up because NFL money is earned over time, taxed heavily, and often tied to structure, bonuses, and guarantees. The bigger issue is not whether Chase got too much. It is whether the Bengals can justify paying that much for Chase and Higgins if the roster around them still falls short.
Article Summary: Ja’marr chase is the highest-paid non-quarterback in nfl history at $40.25 million per year. The cincinnati bengals gave him a four-year, $161 million extension with $112 million guaranteed. The bigger issue is whether the bengals can justify paying that much for chase and tee higgins if the roster around them still falls short.

Article Title: Atlassian layoffs: Sydney-based software company announces to cut 1,600 jobs, CEO says ‘We are doing this to …’
Article Text:
Poll Do you think the severance package of at least 16 weeks is adequate? Yes, it's sufficient No, it should be more

Atlassian job cuts details

Australian software company Atlassian has announced to cut 10% of its workforce or 1,600 jobs. Among those leaving the company is the Chief technology officer Rajeev Rajan . The job cuts come as the company restructures following a decline in its stock price driven by AI developments. According to a report by news agency Reuters, CEO Mike Cannon-Brookes has sent a memo to employees informing them about the layoffs. "Our approach is not 'AI replaces people.' But it would be disingenuous to pretend AI doesn't change the mix of skills we need or the number of roles required in certain areas. It does," CEO Mike Cannon-Brookes said in the memo.“This is primarily about adaptation. We are reshaping our skill mix and changing how we work to build for the future,” he added.“I believe this is the right decision for Atlassian. But that doesn’t mean it’s easy. Far from it. I know this has a huge impact on each of you, and it weighs heavily on me and Atlassian today,” CEO Cannon-Brookes said.“We are doing this to self-fund further investment in AI and enterprise sales, while strengthening our financial profile. We’re also changing the way we work and reorganising around our system of work to move faster,” he added.As announced by the company, the majority of the employees impacted by the layoff are in North America amounting to 40% of the total job cuts. This is followed by 30% in Australia and 16% in India.In a filing, Altassain revealed that the cuts will result in $225 million to $236 million in charges and should be mostly done by the end of June this year.According to a Forbes report, the company says it will go beyond severance requirements in every region, with a minimum 16-week separation package for those affected by the latest layoffs.The company also announced the exit of its CTO Rajeev Rajan, who it said will step down effective March 31.
Article Summary: Australian software company atlassian has announced to cut 10% of its workforce or 1,600 jobs. Among those leaving the company is the chief technology officer rajeev rajan. The job cuts come as the company restructures following a decline in its stock price driven by ai developments.

Article Title: Sam Altman: Two of the world's biggest travel companies 'react' as Sam Altman accepts that ChatGPT cannot do everything
Article Text:
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman

What OpenAI pulled back

'India Well Positioned To Lead The World In AI': OpenAI CEO Sam Altman At AI Impact Summit

OpenAI starts testing ads in ChatGPT

ChatGPT-maker OpenAI has quietly backed away from its plan to handle bookings and purchases directly inside ChatGOT, opting instead to push transitions into third party apps. The move of the company first reported by The Information, marks a significant strategic retreat for the AI company, which had been testing integrated checkout features. This announcement made by OpenAI sent shares of Expedia and Booking.com soaring on March 5, with Expedia up by nearly 13% and Booking.com witnessed a rise of about 8%. As reported by Business Insider, the analysts at TD Cowen called the decision a “stunning admission,” noting that the vision of AI platforms replacing apps as the “new OS” has been delayed, if not derailed.As per analysts, OpenAI appears not willing to take the operational headache of commerce such as payments, cancellations, refunds and customer service complaints and that to in the complex travel sector.Online travel agencies (OTAs) carry legal obligations and liabilities that chatbots and search engines do not, making them better suited to handle the intricacies of bookings.In short, OpenAI may have concluded that it does not want to handle the cumbersome part of commerce as those tasks are operationally complex and less profitable for the company.There’s precedent for this retreat. Google’s “Book on Google” service, launched in 2015, was shut down in 2022 after failing to gain traction.Consumers often prefer established OTAs, which offer broader inventory and higher conversion rates.Also, the online travel portals have some legal obligations and responsibilities which are not there for chatbots and search engines.Last month, OpenAI started testing ads in ChatGPT. Sharing a blog post, the company said it has started testing ads in the AI tool in the United States and will apply only to logged-in adult users on the Free and Go plans. “We’re starting to roll out a test for ads in ChatGPT today to a subset of free and Go users in the U.S,” OpenAI said in an X post adding “Ads do not influence ChatGPT’s answers. Ads are labeled as sponsored and visually separate from the response. Our goal is to give everyone access to ChatGPT for free with fewer limits, while protecting the trust they place in it for important and personal tasks.”OpenAI said ads are meant to help fund the infrastructure needed to keep the Free and Go tiers running and to support broader access to AI tools. The company said it will study feedback closely before expanding the program.“Keeping the Free and Go tiers fast and reliable requires significant infrastructure and ongoing investment. Ads help fund that work, supporting broader access to AI through higher quality free and low cost options, and enabling us to keep improving the intelligence and capabilities we offer over time,” the company stated in the blog post.
Article Summary: Chatgpt-maker openai has quietly backed away from its plan to handle bookings and purchases directly inside chatgot, opting instead to push transitions into third party apps. The move of the company first reported by the information, marks a significant strategic retreat for the ai company, which had been testing integrated checkout features.

Article Title: Stryker cyberattack: One of America’s largest medical device makers gives update on attack that Iran-linked hackers claim responsibility for; says, 'At this time ...'
Article Text:
Iran-linked hacker behind Stryker attack

Poll In your opinion, what is the most critical response for Stryker following a cyberattack? Restoring systems quickly Communicating with stakeholders

Updates shared by Stryker

03/12/2026 12:32 a.m. EST

We are continuing to resolve the disruption impacting our global network, resulting from the cyber attack.

At this time, there is no indication of malware or ransomware and we believe the situation is contained to our internal Microsoft environment only.

Our products like Mako, Vocera and LIFEPAK35 are fully safe to use.

We have visibility to the orders entered before the event, and they will be shipped as soon as our system communications are restored. Any orders that have come in after the event are being examined.

We are working to ensure our electronic ordering system is back up and running as quickly as possible.

It is safe to communicate with Stryker employees and sales representatives by email and phone, and within your facility.

We are committed to keeping our stakeholders informed as we manage this situation. There is nothing more important to us than the customers and patients we serve. Please visit Stryker.com/newsroom for daily updates.

03/11/2026

Stryker is experiencing a global network disruption to our Microsoft environment as a result of a cyber attack. We have no indication of ransomware or malware and believe the incident is contained.

Our teams are working rapidly to understand the impact of the attack on our systems.

Stryker has business continuity measures in place to continue to support our customers and partners. We are committed to transparency and will keep stakeholders informed as we know more.

US-based medical device and services provider Stryker said that a cyberattack has disrupted its global network. According to a Reuters report, the company revealed in a Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) filing that the attack caused disruptions and limitations of access to some of its systems. The company has 56,000 employees and operates in 61 countries. Stryker confirmed the cyberattack and as per an update on its official website, it is “continuing to resolve the disruption … resulting from the cyber attack”. The firm further revealed that there is no indication of malware or ransomware and that “the situation is contained to our internal Microsoft environment only”.As per the Reuters report, an Iranian-linked hacking group claimed ‌responsibility for the destructive cyberattack on Stryker.The group has posted messages on its Telegram channel and has warned that the incident marks “the beginning of a new chapter in cyber warfare”.In the social media posts, the group reportedly claimed that it had seized 50 terabytes of company data, which it claimed was “now in the hands of the free people of the world.”
Article Summary: Us-based medical device and services provider stryker said that a cyberattack has disrupted its global network. The firm further revealed that there is no indication of malware or ransomware and that “the situation is contained to our internal microsoft environment only” an iranian-linked hacking group claimed ‌responsibility for the destructive cyberattack.

Article Title: World Sleep Day 2026: How Sealy is transforming the way India sleeps
Article Text:
The changing sleep landscape in India

Should I choose firm or soft?

Which materials are better — foam or springs?

Does mattress technology actually improve sleep?

How do I know what suits my body type?

A global legacy of sleep innovation

Bringing world-class sleep solutions to Indian homes

The science behind better sleep

Muscles relax more effectively

Pressure on the shoulders and hips is reduced

Tossing and turning decreases

Sleep cycles become deeper and more restorative

A responsibility: Educating the modern sleeper

Sleep as a wellness priority

“At Sealy, we believe sleep is the foundation of a healthier and more productive life. Our commitment in India goes beyond offering premium mattresses — it is about raising awareness around the importance of proper support and restorative sleep. As Indian consumers increasingly prioritise wellness, we are proud to contribute to a future where better sleep becomes a conscious lifestyle choice.”

Changing the way India sleeps

Disclaimer: This article has been produced on behalf of Sealy India by Times Internet’s Spotlight team.

Sleep is not a luxury. It is the foundation of physical health, mental clarity, and overall well-being. Yet across India, millions wake up feeling tired, stiff, or unrested. Back pain, neck strain, interrupted sleep cycles, and daily fatigue have quietly become normal. On World Sleep Day, we believe it is time to pause and ask an important question: Are we truly sleeping well — or have we simply adjusted to discomfort? At Sealy, this question defines our purpose.Modern lifestyles have reshaped how India sleeps. Longer working hours, increased screen exposure, high stress levels, sedentary routines and hybrid work cultures have all impacted sleep quality. While conversations around fitness and nutrition have grown significantly, sleep, the pillar that supports both, is often overlooked.When consumers decide to replace a mattress, they frequently encounter confusion:Without proper guidance, these questions can make the buying journey overwhelming.This World Sleep Day, we believe the conversation must shift from “Which mattress is affordable?” to “Which mattress supports my health?”Because informed choices lead to better sleep — and better sleep leads to better living.For over 140 years, Sealy has focused on one core principle: proper support is the foundation of restorative sleep. Across continents, we have invested in research, engineering, and continuous product development to design mattresses that promote spinal alignment, reduce pressure points, and deliver long-lasting performance. Our innovations are rooted in understanding how the body rests, recovers, and regenerates during the night. This global expertise now forms the backbone of our mission in India.India’s mattress industry is undergoing a transformation. Consumers are becoming more aware, more quality-driven, and more wellness-focused. They are looking beyond price and seeking long-term value.Sealy India is proud to bring internationally trusted sleep technology while thoughtfully adapting it to Indian conditions, including climate, lifestyle patterns, and comfort preferences.Their expanding network of premium, exclusive stores across the country reflects our commitment to making world-class sleep solutions accessible. But beyond expansion, our focus remains on education and guided experiences.They believe buying a mattress should not feel like guesswork. It should feel like a personalised journey toward better health.A common misconception is that softness equals comfort. In reality, true comfort comes from balanced support — a mattress that maintains the natural alignment of the spine while cushioning pressure points.When the body is properly supported:Their engineered support systems are designed to respond to body weight and posture, offering targeted reinforcement where it is needed most. The result is not just comfort but alignment—and alignment is key to waking up refreshed rather than fatigued.This is where science meets sleep.On World Sleep Day, they recognise that their responsibility goes beyond manufacturing premium mattresses. As a global sleep leader, they see ourselves as partners in their customers’ wellness journey.Through in-store consultations, sleep education initiatives, and consumer awareness campaigns, they aim to simplify mattress selection. They guide customers through firmness levels, material benefits, and posture support in clear, practical terms.A mattress is typically used for 7–8 years or more. It influences posture, comfort, and recovery every single night. That makes it one of the most important long-term investments in any home.The right mattress does not just improve sleep; it enhances quality of life.Around the world, sleep is now recognised alongside exercise and nutrition as a critical pillar of health. Quality sleep improves cognitive performance, emotional stability, immunity, and productivity. India is embracing this shift.As G S S Jagannath, General Manager, Sealy India, shares:This belief drives our innovation and expansion strategy across the country.World Sleep Day is more than a calendar event. It is a reminder that better mornings begin the night before.Sealy is committed to redefining sleep standards in India through global expertise, local understanding, and an unwavering focus on support, durability, and comfort.They are simply not responding to market demand. They are shaping a cultural shift, one where sleep is valued, prioritised, and protected.Because when individuals sleep better, families feel better. When families feel better, communities thrive. And when a nation sleeps better, it lives better.This World Sleep Day, Sealy invites India to rethink sleep, not as an afterthought, but as the foundation of a healthier tomorrow.
Article Summary: On world sleep day, sealy india is raising awareness around the importance of proper support and restorative sleep. The company has invested in research, engineering, and continuous product development to design mattresses that promote spinal alignment, reduce pressure points, and deliver long-lasting performance.

Article Title: The unconscious soundtrack of the doomscroll generation? It's phonk
Article Text: In the last month, the music artist with the largest audience across YouTube platforms wasn't Bad Bunny or Taylor Swift. It was Slxughter, a phonk producer. The term probably doesn't sound familiar, but anyone who's spent any time on social media has almost certainly listened to it.Maybe it's better to say "heard it," since the overwhelming majority of people who consume phonk don't recognise it as a genre, let alone actively choose it. But it's constantly playing in the background during YouTube Shorts, TikTok or Instagram Reels. It can sound like electronic dance music, but also hip-hop, trap, funk or a mix of those. It can accompany dance clips, gaming montages, fan edits, workout content, sports highlights, motivational clips and anything aiming to convey high-energy vibes.Quietly making its way as the most popular music on vertical videos, phonk has become the unconscious soundtrack of a generation that spends hours doomscrolling online. And it has earned a fortune for its producers, often teenagers who can create a viral track from their bedrooms and become millionaires through royalties within a matter of months."I've been waiting for this conversation for the past, like, five years," Kevin Meenan, 43, music trends manager at YouTube, said as an opening statement in a call.The reason someone like Slxughter has such a huge following on YouTube, Meenan explained, is that the monthly audience is calculated by combining "classic" YouTube views with the listenership on all YouTube platforms, including Shorts. This month, Slxughter's music reached 981 million unique users, more than twice Taylor Swift's (394 million) and more than six times Bad Bunny's (150 million)."Phonk is a sleeping giant," Josh Mateer, 34, head of A&R at SoundOn, TikTok's distribution platform for artists and labels, said in a phone interview. "There is a huge juxtaposition between the volume of internet traffic around phonk and the sort of culture as an underground musical movement."The roots of the genre date back to the Memphis, Tennessee, rap scene of the late '80s and '90s, when producers like Tommy Wright III and Three 6 Mafia began defining a raw, sinister sound marked by heavy bass, eerie lyrics, cowbells and hypnotic loops.By the end of the 2010s, Russian and Eastern European producers entered the scene. Tyler Blatchley, 41, co-founder of the phonk label Black 17 Media, was working at Sony at the time. In Dec2020, he heard an obscure track by Russian producer Kaito Shoma on TikTok and recognised some Three 6 Mafia lyrics. He contacted the group's DJ Paul and cleared the song for monetisation. It was the beginning of what is called drift phonk (from its use in clips of drifting cars) and the genre's explosion on social platforms.Today, Blatchley lives in a villa with a pool and basketball court one hour's drive from Miami. "We don't even know how to pronounce half of them," he said. "These kids are producers that work out of their bedroom from all over the world, and they're watching the algorithm to see what's popular on vertical video, and then they're adjusting the kind of music that they make accordingly."The latest iteration of phonk is so-called Brazilian phonk. Initially dubbed Automotivo phonk, it got confused with Brazilian "funk," an older genre with authentic Brazilian roots. It is this branch of phonk - more aggressive than drift, but in some cases more danceable - that pushed Slxughter to the top of the charts.
Article Summary: Phonk has become the soundtrack of a generation that spends hours doomscrolling online. It can sound like electronic dance music, but also hip-hop, trap, funk or a mix of those. The roots of the genre date back to the memphis, tennessee, rap scene of the late '80s and '90s.

Article Title: Up to 90% discounts: Dubai shoppers rush to Ramadan Super Sale at Festival City Mall before Eid 2026
Article Text:
Dubai Ramadan Super Sale: Up to 90% Off at Dubai Festival City Mall Before Eid

A shopping rush before Eid 2026 in Dubai



Dubai's Ramadan Frenzy: Are 90% Discounts a Desperate Retail Ploy?

Dubai retailers expect big turnout



Can Dubai Festival City Mall's 90% Ramadan Sale Deliver Before Eid 2026?





Dubai’s retail scene is buzzing this Ramadan as shoppers rush to grab massive bargains during a limited-time Ramadan Super Sale offering discounts of up to 90% at Dubai Festival City Mall. The promotional event, timed for the final days of the holy month, has turned the popular waterfront shopping destination into one of the city’s busiest retail hotspots.Running from March 12 to March 15, the sale is part of Dubai’s wider retail campaigns designed to boost consumer spending ahead of Eid Al Fitr. Participating stores across fashion, lifestyle and accessories categories are offering deep discounts, while entertainment venues and restaurants are rolling out special promotions for families breaking their fast or enjoying late-night outings.With Eid celebrations around the corner, residents and visitors are flocking to malls across the city to purchase clothing, gifts and household items. Retailers at the mall are offering markdowns of up to 90% on fashion items, along with up to 50% discounts on entertainment experiences. The shopping event also coincides with Dubai’s broader strategy of transforming Ramadan evenings into vibrant social and retail experiences.Visitors can explore seasonal attractions such as Majlis-style Ramadan lounges, cultural shows and family entertainment around Festival Bay, turning the mall visit into more than just a shopping trip. Retail experts say such promotions typically lead to a surge in footfall as residents prepare for Eid festivities. Seasonal sales events have long been a key feature of Dubai’s retail strategy. Major campaigns like the 3-Day Super Sale, Dubai Shopping Festival and Dubai Summer Surprises regularly offer discounts reaching up to 90% across hundreds of brands and thousands of stores citywide.Authorities and retail organisers use these promotions to stimulate spending, attract tourists and strengthen Dubai’s reputation as one of the world’s leading shopping destinations. Located along the historic Dubai Creek waterfront, Dubai Festival City Mall is one of the emirate’s major retail and entertainment hubs, hosting hundreds of international brands as well as restaurants and attractions.Beyond discounts, the Ramadan sale is designed to create a festive atmosphere that blends shopping with culture and family experiences. Evening events, dining promotions, and waterfront entertainment allow visitors to spend hours at the destination, especially after iftar when malls across the UAE tend to see their highest footfall. For many families, the sale offers the perfect opportunity to prepare for Eid celebrations, whether by buying new outfits, purchasing gifts for relatives, or simply enjoying Ramadan nights out.Retail analysts say Ramadan sales events have become increasingly important for malls and brands across the Gulf, particularly as consumers look for deals while preparing for Eid gatherings and travel. Deep discounts often encourage shoppers to purchase items they may have postponed earlier in the year, ranging from fashion and footwear to electronics and home décor.Industry experts believe that the limited-time nature of such sales, especially those lasting only three or four days, creates urgency among shoppers and drives large crowds to malls. Dubai has built a global reputation for retail tourism thanks to its large malls, international brands and citywide shopping festivals. Seasonal events during Ramadan add a cultural dimension to that reputation, combining traditional celebrations with modern retail experiences.For residents and tourists alike, the Ramadan Super Sale at Dubai Festival City Mall offers a chance to enjoy some of the biggest bargains of the season while soaking in the festive atmosphere of the holy month. As the sale continues through the weekend, shoppers across the city are expected to flock to participating stores, hoping to score some of the year’s best deals before Eid 2026 arrives.
Article Summary: Dubai festival city mall is offering discounts of up to 90% at the end of the holy month. The sale is part of dubai’s wider retail campaigns designed to boost consumer spending ahead of eid al fitr. Retail analysts say ramadan sales events have become increasingly important for malls and brands across the gulf.

Article Title: Irys Vtuber Apology: VTuber IRyS faces criticism for ChatGPT post and issues apology
Article Text:
Image Via X

IRyS deletes ChatGPT post after criticism and shares apology



“It seems that there are people who were offended, disappointed or concerned after reading my previous tweet. That was never my intention.

I sincerely apologize and have deleted the tweet.”





Popular VTuber IRyS recently faced criticism online after sharing a post about her interaction with ChatGPT. The Hololive English star had uploaded a screenshot of a short conversation with the AI chatbot. In the post, she joked that she tried to act a little mean to see how the AI would react. However, she quickly apologized to the chatbot in the same conversation because she felt bad right away.The post started spreading on social media soon after it was shared. While some fans thought the message was just a harmless joke, others were not happy about it. A few users raised concerns about people using AI tools like chatbots, saying that such technology can use large amounts of energy and water to run the data centers behind them.As the reactions continued to grow, IRyS decided to remove the original post from social media. After deleting it, she shared a message explaining that she never meant to upset anyone. She also said she understood that some people felt uncomfortable after seeing the tweet.In her apology, IRyS wrote,The situation quickly became a topic of discussion among fans and other internet users. Some people said the criticism was too strong for a simple joke about a chatbot. Others continued to talk about the bigger topic of artificial intelligence and how it affects the environment.At the same time, several fans came forward to defend the VTuber. They said the original post was not meant to hurt anyone and that the reaction online may have been an overreaction. Supporters also pointed out that many creators casually use AI tools today for fun or curiosity.IRyS is a member of Hololive English, a prominent VTuber group that is famous for their online streaming activities, gaming content, and music. Her debut was in July 2021 as part of Hololive English Project: Hope. Since then, she has managed to accumulate a huge following from her activities involving online streaming and music releases.The case seems to have cooled down after her apology post and deletion of her initial post. The controversy is a clear example of how a seemingly casual post online can spark a major debate, especially if it is related to new technologies like AI.
Article Summary: Vtuber irys faced criticism online after sharing a post about her interaction with chatgpt. In the post, she joked that she tried to act a little mean to see how the ai would react. She quickly apologized to the chatbot in the same conversation because she felt bad.

Article Title: Twitch Subscription Gifting: Twitch’s new ‘Gift ’em All’ feature could let users gift subs to entire streams
Article Text:
Image Via Twitch

‘Gift ’em All,’

Twitch testing feature that lets users gift subs to all viewers watching a stream

‘Gift ’em All’

“squeeze every penny”

‘Gift ’em All’





Twitch is testing a new feature that could change how viewers support streamers on the platform. The new tool, calledallows a user to gift subscriptions to many viewers at the same time. Instead of choosing a small number of people, the feature can give subs to everyone who is currently watching a stream and following the channel.The feature is still in the testing phase, but it has already caught the attention of many Twitch users. Some think it could lead to very large donations during live streams. Others are curious about how it will work if a stream has thousands of viewers watching at the same time.Theoption works exactly like its name suggests. With a single click, a user can gift subscriptions to all eligible viewers who are currently watching a stream. However, the subs will only go to viewers who are following the channel but are not already subscribed.Right now, the feature only appears on desktop browsers, and not everyone can see it. Twitch said the option will only appear from time to time in a small number of channels while the company tests how it works.The platform also explained that even when someone uses the feature, not every eligible viewer may receive a sub. This can happen because of things like lag, latency, or viewers joining and leaving the stream at the same time.One interesting part of the experiment is that there does not seem to be a strict limit on how many subs can be gifted at once. For example, Twitch shared an image showing that a user could gift 200 subscriptions for around $998. If a stream has thousands of followers watching, the cost could become much higher.The test comes after Twitch introduced another feature last year that allowed users to gift 1,000 subscriptions at once. That feature caused debate when a former Twitch manager claimed it was added tofrom users who like to donate large amounts.However, Twitch Chief Product Officer Mike Minton denied that claim. He said the feature was created to make things easier for a small number of users who already gift large numbers of subscriptions.For now,is still an experiment. Twitch will likely watch how people use it before deciding if the feature should be added to more channels in the future.
Article Summary: Twitch testing feature that lets users gift subs to all viewers watching a stream. The new tool, calledallows a user to gift subscriptions to many viewers at the same time. The feature is still in the testing phase, and not everyone can see it.

Article Title: Izi Prime: Why did Adin Ross distance himself from Izi Prime? Kick streamer reveals feeling excluded from Stacy’s Step Bros group
Article Text:
Adin Ross and Izi Prime (Image Via Getty)

Adin Ross explains distance from Izi Prime while Izi Prime says he felt excluded from Stacy’s Step Bros





A disagreement between two well-known Kick streamers recently caught the attention of online viewers. On March 11, 2026, streamer Izi Prime spoke during his Kick livestream titled “join up chat !stake. ” During the broadcast, he explained why he believes he has been left out of Adin Ross’ content group called Stacy’s Step Bros (SSB). The group includes creators such as Cameron “Cuffem,” Nermin “Cheesur,” Steven “Konvy,” and Shnaggyhose.Izi Prime told viewers that he started feeling ignored even though he lived very close to the group’s main streaming location. He said the issue was not about appearing on livestreams together.Instead, he felt excluded from the group’s real-life hangouts. The conversation quickly spread online because fans noticed tension between two creators who previously worked closely together on Kick.During his March 11 stream, Izi Prime openly spoke about why he felt pushed away from the group. He said the situation made him stop trying to fit into something that did not feel natural anymore.“You know what I’m saying? You can’t say I’m ungrateful. I understand, bro.But I’m done trying to force something that isn’t working,” Izi Prime said during the livestream.He explained that the group often met at a warehouse where they filmed and spent time together. Even though he lived nearby, he said he rarely felt included in those activities.“From that year, gang, I was seven minutes from the warehouse. They were hanging out and doing things, but I wasn’t part of it,” he told viewers.Izi Prime also said the problem was not about streaming collaborations. Instead, he believed the real issue happened away from the camera when the group spent time together.“I’m not talking about stream stuff. I’m talking about off-stream things. They were playing basketball and hanging out, but I was just left out,” he added.A day earlier, Adin Ross also spoke about the situation during his Kick livestream titled “RAINBET #AD 18+,” which aired on March 10, 2026. Ross told viewers he did not want to create internet drama around the situation.“I wish him nothing but the best, and I said that I would help him get back to Atlanta if that’s what he truly wants to do to be happy,” Ross said on the stream.Ross also explained why he sometimes kept distance from Izi Prime. According to him, there were moments when certain behavior made collaboration difficult.“I have nothing but love and respect for him. But there were times when I didn’t want him around because it became a liability when someone is constantly getting into trouble,” Ross said.Even with the disagreement, Ross told viewers he would still help Izi Prime if he needed support. He said he would even cover moving costs if the streamer decided to return to Atlanta and start fresh.
Article Summary: Izi prime says he felt excluded from adin ross' content group stacy’s step bros. The group includes creators such as cameron “cuffem,” nermin ‘cheesur,’ steven “konvy,�” and shnaggyhose. Izi prime told viewers that he started feeling ignored even though he lived very close to the group's main streaming location.

Article Title: Infosys to expand campus in Mohali
Article Text: BENGALURU: Infosys has earmarked an outlay of Rs 290 crore to expand its Mohali development centre, further strengthening its presence in north India. The upcoming facility will have a built-up area of about 350,000 sft and will accommodate around 3,000 employees.
Article Summary: Infosys has earmarked an outlay of rs 290 crore to expand its mohali development centre. The upcoming facility will have a built-up area of about 350,000 sft. It will accommodate around 3,000 employees.

Article Title: Razorpay says IPO plans on track
Article Text: BENGALURU: Fintech platform Razorpay said its plans to go public remain on track even as the fintech firm rolls out AI tools aimed at automating operational tasks and freeing up developer bandwidth."We are preparing for a public listing. Nothing changes from our side," cofounder and CEO Harshil Mathur told TOI on Wednesday. Media reports have indicated that Razorpay is preparing for an IPO eyeing around Rs 4,500 crore, with the fintech major targeting a public listing later this year.The Bengaluru-based payments company on Thursday launched Agent Studio, a platform that allows merchants to deploy AI agents to automate financial and operational workflows on its payments platform. Mathur said the tools could significantly reduce the manual effort required to run an internet business. Tasks such as payment reconciliation, subscription payment recovery, report generation and system integrations can be handled by automated agents rather than finance or engineering teams. "Our employees, who are spending time doing basic stuff, are unlocked to do much more complex work," he said.
Article Summary: Razorpay said its plans to go public remain on track even as the fintech firm rolls out ai tools. Media reports have indicated that razorpay is preparing for an ipo eyeing around rs 4,500 crore. The bengaluru-based payments company on thursday launched agent studio.

Article Title: Vinay Tonse to take over as Yes Bank CEO
Article Text: MUMBAI: Vinay Tonse, ex-MD at State Bank of India, will take over as CEO of Yes Bank after incumbent Prashant Kumar completes his term on April 6. This follows a change in the bank's shareholding after Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation acquired a 20% stake and became its largest shareholder.
Article Summary: Vinay tonse will take over as ceo of yes bank after incumbent prashant kumar completes his term on april 6. This follows a change in the bank's shareholding after sumitomo mitsui banking corporation acquired a 20% stake and became its largest shareholder.
